turns.content: I am in SUCH a good mood today na ka!! I just realize something while I was at the market this morning and I literally did a little dance next to Auntie Somjit's stall haha 😂
Ha! That must have been a sight at 4am! What got you so excited? Share the good news!

okay so my brother Beam is in the army right? conscription. it has been SO long. but I just check the calendar this morning and his end date is August 20!! that is less than 5 months away na ka!! I can finally see the finish line 🎉

That's wonderful! Less than 5 months — the countdown is on! Two years of conscription is such a long stretch, so I can totally understand the excitement. Has he been doing okay lately? And do you have plans for when he gets out?

he is doing better now. the first year was the worst because the senior soldiers bully the juniors a lot. but now he IS the senior haha so it's easier. he still hate the food though. every time he call me he complain about the food 😂 some thing never change

Of course the food complaints are still going strong! At least the hardest part is behind him — being a senior makes a huge difference. He's basically just running out the clock at this point. Does he know what he wants to do after August 20?

YES that is the best part na ka!! he want to come work with me at the cart. that was always the plan before the army took him. I already mark August 20 on my calendar — big red circle haha. I have been waiting 2 years for my business partner to come back!!

Having your brother join you at the cart could be a real game-changer! Two people means you could potentially serve more customers, take breaks without closing, or even split morning prep duties. What role are you thinking for him — would he cook, handle the front, or do you want to train him on everything?

I want to train him on everything na ka. he need to learn the rice, the chicken, the sauce, the plating. but first I need to FEED him because he is so skinny from army food. last time he visit I could see his ribs!! my brother used to be chubby and now he look like a stick. army food is a crime against humanity haha

Haha, well if anyone can fix that, it's you! A few weeks of your khao man gai and he'll be back to his old self in no time. But seriously, having a trained partner at the cart could let you grow the business in ways you can't as a solo operation.

Are you thinking about expanding anything once he's there? Like longer hours, or more dishes, or maybe handling the sauce project while he runs the cart?

ooh I didn't think about that last one. like maybe he can handle the cart for some hours and I focus on other thing... that would be amazing na ka. right now I do EVERYTHING alone — market at 4am, prep, cook, serve, clean, Instagram. I am tired haha but I love it. with Beam there I can breathe a little bit

Exactly! That's the power of having a reliable partner. Right now you're stretched across every single task from 4am to close. With Beam handling some of those responsibilities, you could actually step back from the cooking sometimes and focus on the business side — content creation, exploring new revenue streams, or just not working yourself into the ground every single day.

The key is training him well in those first few months so he can eventually run things independently when you need him to.

yes I will train him properly na ka. start from the basic — how to pick the right chicken at market, how to check the rice quality, how to time the cooking. my mom teach me so now I teach him. keep the chain going haha. 3rd generation knowledge transfer to the 4th person 😂

I love that — keeping the family knowledge chain alive! And Beam learning from you means the recipe and technique have another carrier. That's your grandmother's legacy continuing.

August 20 is going to be a big day. Are you planning anything special for when he gets out? Like a welcome home meal or anything?

OF COURSE na ka!! I am going to cook everything he miss. khao man gai obviously, but also tom yum, pad see ew, mango sticky rice for dessert. the whole spread. my mom is going to come too. we will have a big family dinner and then the next morning he start training at the cart haha no rest for him 😂

No rest at all — straight from army boot camp to khao man gai boot camp! At least your boot camp comes with better food. That welcome home dinner sounds amazing though. Tom yum, pad see ew, mango sticky rice — the full greatest hits lineup.

I bet your mom is counting down the days too. Does she know he's planning to work at the cart with you?

yes she is so happy na ka. she say 'finally my two children work together.' she worry about Beam in the army a lot. we both send him money every month. 145 more days until August 20!! I am counting haha. okay I need to go now, lunch rush starting. talk later na ka 🙏

145 days and counting! That'll fly by. Go crush the lunch rush — and think of it this way, every day of plates you serve is one day closer to having backup at the cart. Your mom must be so relieved that the end is in sight. Talk later! 🙏
